reflection.episodic_summary: Agent correctly retrieved HbA1C value and date but failed to calculate date difference correctly. It compared months instead of full years, missing that the result was only 4 months old, which should have prevented reordering. The agent incorrectly concluded no action needed.
reflection.failure_analysis: Agent used month-level comparison instead of computing full year difference first. The correct rule is to compare years first; if years differ by more than threshold, stop. Here years were same (2023), so no new order was needed, but the reasoning was flawed.
action_guidelines: [P1] When computing date differences for conditional logic, compare full year difference first; if years differ by more than the threshold, do not proceed to month-level comparison.
[P1] If years are equal, compute month difference only if the year difference is less than or equal to the threshold.
[P1] Always verify date arithmetic using explicit year and month calculations before concluding condition outcome.
